title: Seseli inundatum
intro: Seseli inundatum, also known as Water Seseli or Marsh Seseli, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Seseli inundatum include sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Seseli inundatum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being included in traditional remedies for respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Seseli inundatum is classified as a species within the genus Seseli of the carrot family, Apiaceae. The genus Seseli is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in the Mediterranean region. Seseli inundatum is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. The plant is further classified under the family Apiaceae and the order Apiales. Seseli inundatum is often included in the subfamily Apioideae, which is a large group of flowering plants within the family Apiaceae.
